name: ACM-1A: Manage Asset Inventory
practice_description: There is an inventory of OT and IT assets that are important to the delivery of the function; management of the inventory may be ad hoc

name: ACM-1B: Manage Asset Inventory
practice_description: There is an inventory of information assets that are important to the delivery of the function (e.g., SCADA set points, customer information, financial data); management of the inventory may be ad hoc

name: ACM-1C: Manage Asset Inventory
practice_description: Inventory attributes include information to support the Cyber Security strategy (e.g., location, asset owner, applicable Security requirements, service dependencies, service level agreements, and conformance of assets to relevant industry standards)

name: ACM-1D: Manage Asset Inventory
practice_description: Inventoried assets are prioritised based on their importance to the delivery of the function

name: ACM-1E: Manage Asset Inventory
practice_description: There is an inventory for all connected IT and OT assets related to the delivery of the function

name: ACM-1F: Manage Asset Inventory
practice_description: The asset inventory is current (as defined by the organisation)

name: ACM-2A: Manage Asset Configuration
practice_description: Configuration baselines are established, at least in an ad hoc manner, for inventoried assets where it is desirable to ensure that multiple assets are configured similarly

name: ACM-2B: Manage Asset Configuration
practice_description: Configuration baselines are used, at least in an ad hoc manner, to configure assets at deployment

name: ACM-2C: Manage Asset Configuration
practice_description: The design of configuration baselines includes Cyber Security objectives

name: ACM-2D: Manage Asset Configuration
practice_description: Configuration of assets are monitored for consistency with baselines throughout the assets' life cycle

name: ACM-2E: Manage Asset Configuration
practice_description: Configuration baselines are reviewed and updated at an organisationally-defined frequency

name: ACM-3A: Manage Changes to Assets
practice_description: Changes to inventoried assets are evaluated, at least in an ad hoc manner, before being implemented

name: ACM-3B: Manage Changes to Assets
practice_description: Changes to inventoried assets are logged, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: ACM-3C: Manage Changes to Assets
practice_description: Changes to assets are tested prior to being deployed, whenever possible

name: ACM-3D: Manage Changes to Assets
practice_description: Change management practices address the full life cycle of assets (i.e., acquisition, deployment, operation, retirement)

name: ACM-3E: Manage Changes to Assets
practice_description: Changes to assets are tested for Cyber Security impact prior to being deployed

name: ACM-3F: Manage Changes to Assets
practice_description: Change logs include information about modifications that impact the Cyber Security requirements of assets (availability, integrity, confidentiality)

name: ACM-AP1: ACM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Changes to Internet-facing assets are not assessed or tested to identify potential cyber security vulnerabilities arising from the change itself, prior to implementation

name: ACM-AP2: ACM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: The management of asset inventories is not linked to data governance or business impact assessment activities

name: ACM-AP3: ACM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Asset inventories have not been updated in the past 24 months

name: APM-1A: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: Privacy requirements applicable to the organisation have been identified, even in an ad-hoc manner.

name: APM-1B: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: The organisation has defined what it considers personal information in the context of its business activities, even in an ad-hoc manner.

name: APM-1C: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: There is a point of contact (person or role) to whom privacy issues could be reported, even in an ad-hoc manner.

name: APM-1D: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: Business activities which involve the collection, processing, storage or transmission of personal information have been identified

name: APM-1E: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: The organisation's personal information holdings are documented

name: APM-1F: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: A privacy policy has been documented and communicated within the organisation and the general public

name: APM-1G: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: The organisation's requirements for handling of personal information have been defined within the privacy policy

name: APM-1H: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: Specific roles and accountabilities have been assigned for privacy management within the organisation

name: APM-1I: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: A privacy management plan has been implemented to govern the organisation's ongoing compliance with applicable privacy requirements

name: APM-1J: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: Privacy related risks have been identified, assessed and documented in a risk register

name: APM-1K: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: A documented process exists for responding to privacy enquiries and complaints, including customer correction of their personal information

name: APM-1L: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: The organisation provides privacy training to staff responsible for handling personal information

name: APM-1M: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: Existing incident response plan specifically consider data breach scenarios involving personal information

name: APM-1N: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: Incident response plans for data breach scenarios are tested periodically and updated based on improvement opportunities identified

name: APM-1O: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: The organisation's compliance with applicable privacy requirements is periodically assessed and reported to senior management

name: APM-1P: Manage personal information and privacy
practice_description: The privacy management plan is periodically updated to reflect the changing threat and regulatory environment

name: APM-AP1: APM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: The function is unaware whether personal information is collected

name: CPM-1A: Establish Cyber Security Program Strategy
practice_description: The organisation has a Cyber Security program strategy, which may be developed and managed in an ad hoc manner

name: CPM-1B: Establish Cyber Security Program Strategy
practice_description: The Cyber Security program strategy defines objectives for the organisation's Cyber Security activities

name: CPM-1C: Establish Cyber Security Program Strategy
practice_description: The Cyber Security program strategy and priorities are documented and aligned with the organisation's strategic objectives and risk to critical infrastructure

name: CPM-1D: Establish Cyber Security Program Strategy
practice_description: The Cyber Security program strategy defines the organisation's approach to provide program oversight and governance for Cyber Security activities

name: CPM-1E: Establish Cyber Security Program Strategy
practice_description: The Cyber Security program strategy defines the structure and organisation of the Cyber Security program

name: CPM-1F: Establish Cyber Security Program Strategy
practice_description: The Cyber Security program strategy is approved by senior management

name: CPM-1G: Establish Cyber Security Program Strategy
practice_description: The Cyber Security program strategy is updated to reflect business changes, changes in the operating environment, and changes in the threat profile (TVM-1d)

name: CPM-2A: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: Resources (people, tools, and funding) are provided, at least in an ad hoc manner, to support the Cyber Security program

name: CPM-2B: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: Senior management provides sponsorship for the Cyber Security program, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: CPM-2C: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: The Cyber Security program is established according to the Cyber Security program strategy

name: CPM-2D: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: Adequate funding and other resources (i.e., people and tools) are provided to establish and operate a Cyber Security program aligned with the program strategy

name: CPM-2E: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: Senior management sponsorship for the Cyber Security program is visible and active (e.g., the importance and value of Cyber Security activities is regularly communicated by senior management)

name: CPM-2F: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: If the organisation develops or procures software, secure software development practices are sponsored as an element of the Cyber Security program

name: CPM-2G: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: The development and maintenance of Cyber Security policies is sponsored

name: CPM-2H: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: Responsibility for the Cyber Security program is assigned to a role with requisite authority

name: CPM-2I: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: The performance of the Cyber Security program is monitored to ensure it aligns with the Cyber Security program strategy

name: CPM-2J: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: The Cyber Security program is independently reviewed (i.e., by reviewers who are not in the program) for achievement of Cyber Security program objectives

name: CPM-2K: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: The Cyber Security program addresses and enables the achievement of regulatory compliance as appropriate

name: CPM-2L: Sponsor Cyber Security Program
practice_description: The Cyber Security program monitors and/or participates in selected industry Cyber Security standards or initiatives

name: CPM-3A: Establish and Maintain Cyber Security Architecture
capability: Establish and Maintain Cyber Security Architecture
practice_description: A strategy to architecturally isolate the organisation's IT systems from OT systems is implemented, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: CPM-3B: Establish and Maintain Cyber Security Architecture
capability: Establish and Maintain Cyber Security Architecture
practice_description: A Cyber Security architecture is in place to enable segmentation, isolation, and other requirements that support the Cyber Security strategy

name: CPM-3C: Establish and Maintain Cyber Security Architecture
capability: Establish and Maintain Cyber Security Architecture
practice_description: Architectural segmentation and isolation is maintained according to a documented plan

name: CPM-3D: Establish and Maintain Cyber Security Architecture
capability: Establish and Maintain Cyber Security Architecture
practice_description: Cyber Security architecture is updated at an organisation-defined frequency to keep it current

name: CPM-4A: Perform Secure Software Development
practice_description: Software to be deployed on assets that are important to the delivery of the function is developed using secure software development practices

name: CPM-4B: Perform Secure Software Development
practice_description: Policies require that software that is to be deployed on assets that are important to the delivery of the function be developed using secure software development practices

name: CPM-AP1: CPM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Operational assets can route traffic directly to the Internet

name: CPM-AP2: CPM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Remote or third-party access to assets circumvents network security controls

name: CPM-AP3: CPM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Critical assets cannot be isolated from non-critical assets in response to a cyber security threat or incident

name: EDM-1A: Identify Dependencies
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Important IT and OT supplier dependencies are identified (i.e., external parties on which the delivery of the function depend, including operating partners), at least in an ad hoc manner

name: EDM-1B: Identify Dependencies
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Important customer dependencies are identified (i.e., external parties that are dependent on the delivery of the function including operating partners), at least in an ad hoc manner

name: EDM-1C: Identify Dependencies
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Supplier dependencies are identified according to established criteria

name: EDM-1D: Identify Dependencies
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Customer dependencies are identified according to established criteria

name: EDM-1E: Identify Dependencies
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Single-source and other essential dependencies are identified

name: EDM-1F: Identify Dependencies
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Dependencies are prioritised

name: EDM-1G: Identify Dependencies
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Dependency prioritisation and identification are based on the function's or organisation's risk criteria (RM-1c)

name: EDM-2A: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Significant Cyber Security risks due to suppliers and other dependencies are identified and addressed, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: EDM-2B: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Cyber Security requirements are considered, at least in an ad hoc manner, when establishing relationships with suppliers and other third parties

name: EDM-2C: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Identified Cyber Security dependency risks are entered into the risk register (RM-2j)

name: EDM-2D: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Contracts and agreements with third parties incorporate sharing of Cyber Security threat information

name: EDM-2E: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Cyber Security requirements are established for suppliers according to a defined practice, including requirements for secure software development practices where appropriate

name: EDM-2F: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Agreements with suppliers and other external entities include Cyber Security requirements

name: EDM-2G: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Evaluation and selection of suppliers and other external entities includes consideration of their ability to meet Cyber Security requirements

name: EDM-2H: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Agreements with suppliers require notification of Cyber Security incidents related to the delivery of the product or service

name: EDM-2I: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Suppliers and other external entities are periodically reviewed for their ability to continually meet the Cyber Security requirements

name: EDM-2J: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Cyber Security risks due to external dependencies are managed according to the organisation's risk management criteria and process

name: EDM-2K: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Cyber Security requirements are established for supplier dependencies based on the organisation's risk criteria (RM-1c)

name: EDM-2L: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Agreements with suppliers require notification of vulnerability-inducing product defects throughout the intended life cycle of delivered products

name: EDM-2M: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Acceptance testing of procured assets includes testing for Cyber Security requirements

name: EDM-2N: Manage Dependency Risk
domain: Supply Chain and External Dependencies Management
practice_description: Information sources are monitored to identify and avoid supply chain threats (e.g., counterfeit parts, software, and services)

name: IAM-1A: Establish and Maintain Identities
practice_description: Identities are provisioned, at least in an ad hoc manner, for personnel and other entities (e.g., services, devices) who require access to assets (note that this does not preclude shared identities)

name: IAM-1B: Establish and Maintain Identities
practice_description: Credentials are issued, at least in an ad hoc manner, for personnel and other entities that require access to assets (e.g., passwords, smart cards, certificates, keys)

name: IAM-1C: Establish and Maintain Identities
practice_description: Identities are deprovisioned, at least in an ad hoc manner, when no longer required

name: IAM-1D: Establish and Maintain Identities
practice_description: Identity repositories are periodically reviewed and updated to ensure validity (i.e., to ensure that the identities still need access)

name: IAM-1E: Establish and Maintain Identities
practice_description: Credentials are periodically reviewed to ensure that they are associated with the correct person or entity

name: IAM-1F: Establish and Maintain Identities
practice_description: Identities are deprovisioned within organisationally defined time thresholds when no longer required

name: IAM-1G: Establish and Maintain Identities
practice_description: Requirements for credentials are informed by the organisation's risk criteria (e.g., multifactor credentials for higher risk access) (RM-1c)

name: IAM-2A: Control Access
practice_description: Access requirements, including those for remote access, are determined

name: IAM-2B: Control Access
practice_description: Access is granted to identities, at least in an ad hoc manner, based on requirements

name: IAM-2C: Control Access
practice_description: Access is revoked, at least in an ad hoc manner, when no longer required

name: IAM-2D: Control Access
practice_description: Access requirements incorporate least privilege and separation of duties principles

name: IAM-2E: Control Access
practice_description: Access requests are reviewed and approved by the asset owner

name: IAM-2F: Control Access
practice_description: Root privileges, administrative access, emergency access, and shared accounts receive additional scrutiny and monitoring

name: IAM-2G: Control Access
practice_description: Access privileges are reviewed and updated to ensure validity, at an organisationally defined frequency

name: IAM-2H: Control Access
practice_description: Access to assets is granted by the asset owner based on risk to the function

name: IAM-2I: Control Access
practice_description: Anomalous access attempts are monitored as indicators of Cyber Security events

name: IAM-AP1: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Identities (users) are created, and access to assets is provisioned, before confirming if the identity (user) has a genuine need for access

name: IAM-AP2: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: A complete and current register of identities (users) with privileged access is not maintained

name: IAM-AP3: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Identity (user) deprovisioning is not informed and supported by organisational risk criteria (RM-1C)

name: IAM-AP4: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Non-public, Internet-facing assets can be accessed using single-factor authentication

name: IAM-AP5: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Privileged access to one or more assets is provisioned by default

name: IAM-AP6: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Identities (users) have been provisioned with access to assets which breaches a segregation of duties requirement

name: IAM-AP7: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Identities (users) cannot be individually identified and attributed to a person

name: IAM-AP8: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Unusual or suspicious access to assets is not monitored by security monitoring solutions

name: IAM-AP9: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Unknown or unauthorised identities (users) and assets can connect to known assets

name: IAM-AP10: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: The continued need for an identity (user) to have access to an asset is not validated when identity (user) repositories are reviewed

name: IAM-AP11: IAM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Identities (users) are not prohibited (by organisational policy) from connecting to critical assets using unknown or unauthorised assets

name: IR-1A: Detect Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: There is a point of contact (person or role) to whom Cyber Security events could be reported

name: IR-1B: Detect Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Detected Cyber Security events are reported, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: IR-1C: Detect Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Cyber Security events are logged and tracked, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: IR-1D: Detect Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Criteria are established for Cyber Security event detection (e.g., what constitutes an event, where to look for events)

name: IR-1E: Detect Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: There is a repository where Cyber Security events are logged based on the established criteria

name: IR-1F: Detect Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Event information is correlated to support incident analysis by identifying patterns, trends, and other common features

name: IR-1G: Detect Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Cyber Security event detection activities are adjusted based on information from the organisation's risk register (RM-2j) and threat profile (TVM-1d) to help detect known threats and monitor for identified risks

name: IR-1H: Detect Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: The common operating picture for the function is monitored to support the identification of Cyber Security events (SA-3a)

name: IR-2A: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
practice_description: Criteria for Cyber Security event escalation are established, including Cyber Security incident declaration criteria, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: IR-2B: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
practice_description: Cyber Security events are analysed, at least in an ad hoc manner, to support escalation and the declaration of Cyber Security incidents

name: IR-2C: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
practice_description: Escalated Cyber Security events and incidents are logged and tracked, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: IR-2D: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
practice_description: Criteria for Cyber Security event escalation, including Cyber Security incident criteria, are established based on the potential impact to the function

name: IR-2E: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
practice_description: Criteria for Cyber Security event escalation, including Cyber Security incident declaration criteria, are updated at an organisation-defined frequency

name: IR-2F: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
practice_description: There is a repository where escalated Cyber Security events and Cyber Security incidents are logged and tracked to closure

name: IR-2G: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
practice_description: Criteria for Cyber Security event escalation, including Cyber Security incident declaration criteria, are adjusted according to information from the organisation's risk register (RM-2j) and threat profile (TVM-1d)

name: IR-2H: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
practice_description: Escalated Cyber Security events and declared Cyber Security incidents inform the common operating picture (SA-3a) for the function

name: IR-2I: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Escalate Cyber Security Events and Declare Incidents
practice_description: Escalated Cyber Security events and declared incidents are correlated to support the discovery of patterns, trends, and other common features

name: IR-3A: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident response personnel are identified and roles are assigned, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: IR-3B: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Responses to escalated Cyber Security events and incidents are implemented, at least in an ad hoc manner, to limit impact to the function and restore normal operations

name: IR-3C: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Reporting of escalated Cyber Security events and incidents is performed (e.g., internal reporting, ACSC), at least in an ad hoc manner

name: IR-3D: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident response is performed according to defined procedures that address all phases of the incident life cycle (e.g., triage, handling, communication, coordination, and closure)

name: IR-3E: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident response plans are exercised at an organisation- defined frequency

name: IR-3F: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident response plans address OT and IT assets important to the delivery of the function

name: IR-3G: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Training is conducted for Cyber Security event and incident response teams

name: IR-3H: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident root-cause analysis and lessons-learned activities are performed, and corrective actions are taken

name: IR-3I: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident responses are coordinated with law enforcement and other government entities as appropriate, including support for evidence collection and preservation

name: IR-3J: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident response personnel participate in joint Cyber Security exercises with other organisations (e.g., table top, simulated incidents)

name: IR-3K: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident response plans are reviewed and updated at an organisation-defined frequency

name: IR-3L: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident response activities are coordinated with relevant external entities

name: IR-3M: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Cyber Security event and incident response plans are aligned with the function's risk criteria (RM-1c) and threat profile (TVM-1d)

name: IR-3N: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Policy and procedures for reporting Cyber Security event and incident information to designated authorities conform with applicable laws, regulations, and contractual agreements

name: IR-3O: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
capability: Respond to Incidents and Escalated Cyber Security Events
practice_description: Restored assets are configured appropriately and inventory information is updated following execution of response plans

name: IR-4A: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: The activities necessary to sustain minimum operations of the function are identified, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: IR-4B: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: The sequence of activities necessary to return the function to normal operation is identified, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: IR-4C: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Continuity plans are developed, at least in an ad hoc manner, to sustain and restore operation of the function

name: IR-4D: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Business impact analyses inform the development of continuity plans

name: IR-4E: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO) for the function are incorporated into continuity plans

name: IR-4F: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Continuity plans are evaluated and exercised

name: IR-4G: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Business impact analyses are periodically reviewed and updated

name: IR-4H: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO) are aligned with the function's risk criteria (RM-1c)

name: IR-4I: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: The results of continuity plan testing and/or activation are compared to recovery objectives, and plans are improved accordingly

name: IR-4J: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Continuity plans are periodically reviewed and updated

name: IR-4K: Plan for Continuity
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Restored assets are configured appropriately and inventory information is updated following execution of continuity plans

name: IR-AP1: IR Anti-Patterns
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Critical functions have not been identified

name: IR-AP2: IR Anti-Patterns
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Services and assets that support the delivery of critical functions have not been identified (IR-AP1)

name: IR-AP3: IR Anti-Patterns
domain: Event and Incident Response, Continuity of Operations
practice_description: Incident responders do not know which authorities (including law enforcement) should be contacted or how to contact them

name: ISC-1A: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Information is collected from and provided to selected individuals and/or organisations, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: ISC-1B: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Responsibility for Cyber Security reporting obligations are assigned to personnel (e.g., internal reporting to management, external reporting to government (e.g. ACSC) or law enforcement (e.g. AFP), at least in an ad hoc manner

name: ISC-1C: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Information-sharing stakeholders are identified based on their relevance to the continued operation of the function (e.g., connected utilities, vendors, sector organisations, regulators, internal entities)

name: ISC-1D: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Information is collected from and provided to identified information-sharing stakeholders

name: ISC-1E: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Technical sources are identified that can be consulted on Cyber Security issues

name: ISC-1F: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Provisions are established and maintained to enable secure sharing of sensitive or classified information

name: ISC-1G: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Information-sharing practices address both standard operations and emergency operations

name: ISC-1H: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Information-sharing stakeholders are identified based on shared interest in and risk to critical infrastructure

name: ISC-1I: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: The function or the organisation participates with information sharing and analysis centres

name: ISC-1J: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Information-sharing requirements have been defined for the function and address timely dissemination of Cyber Security information

name: ISC-1K: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: Procedures are in place to analyse and de-conflict received information

name: ISC-1L: Share Cyber Security Information
practice_description: A network of internal and external trust relationships (formal and/or informal) has been established to vet and validate information about Cyber events

name: RM-1A: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
capability: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
practice_description: There is a documented Cyber Security risk management strategy

name: RM-1B: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
capability: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
practice_description: The Cyber Security risk management strategy provides an approach for risk prioritisation, including consideration of impact

name: RM-1C: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
capability: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
practice_description: Organisational risk criteria (objective criteria that the organisation uses for evaluating, categorising, and prioritising operational risks based on impact, tolerance for risk, and risk response approaches) are defined and available

name: RM-1D: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
capability: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
practice_description: The Cyber Security risk management strategy is periodically updated to reflect the current threat environment

name: RM-1E: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
capability: Establish Cyber Security Risk Management Strategy
practice_description: An organisation-specific risk taxonomy is documented and is used in risk management activities

name: RM-2A: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: Cyber Security risks are identified, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: RM-2B: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: Identified Cyber Security risks are mitigated, accepted, tolerated, or transferred, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: RM-2C: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: Cyber Security risk assessments are performed to identify risks in accordance with the risk management strategy

name: RM-2D: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: Identified Cyber Security risks are documented

name: RM-2E: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: Identified Cyber Security risks are analysed to prioritise response activities in accordance with the Cyber Security risk management strategy

name: RM-2F: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: Identified Cyber Security risks are monitored in accordance with the Cyber Security risk management strategy

name: RM-2G: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: Cyber Security risk analysis is informed by network (IT and/or OT) architecture

name: RM-2H: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: The Cyber Security risk management program defines and operates risk management policies and procedures that implement the Cyber Security risk management strategy

name: RM-2I: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: A current Cyber Security architecture is used to inform Cyber Security risk analysis

name: RM-2J: Manage Cyber Security Risk
practice_description: A Cyber Security risk register (a structured repository of identified risks) is used to support Cyber Security risk management activities

name: RM-AP1: RM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Identified risks are not periodically reviewed

name: RM-AP2: RM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Identified cyber security risks remain untreated for long periods of time

name: RM-AP3: RM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Assets are risk-assessed in isolation. Interdependencies with other assets are not considered

name: RM-AP4: RM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Cyber security risk management activities are not informed and supported by organisational risk criteria (RM-1C)

name: SA-1A: Perform Logging
practice_description: Logging is occurring, at least in an ad hoc manner, for assets important to the function, where possible

name: SA-1B: Perform Logging
practice_description: Logging requirements have been defined for all assets important to the function (e.g., scope of activity and coverage of assets, Cyber Security requirements [confidentiality, integrity, availability])

name: SA-1C: Perform Logging
practice_description: Log data are being aggregated within the function

name: SA-1D: Perform Logging
practice_description: Logging requirements are based on the risk to the function

name: SA-1E: Perform Logging
practice_description: Log data support other business and Security processes (e.g., incident response, asset management)

name: SA-2A: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Cyber Security monitoring activities are performed (e.g., periodic reviews of log data), at least in an ad hoc manner

name: SA-2B: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Operational environments are monitored, at least in an ad hoc manner, for anomalous behaviour that may indicate a Cyber Security event

name: SA-2C: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Monitoring and analysis requirements have been defined for the function and address timely review of event data

name: SA-2D: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Alarms and alerts are configured to aid in the identification of Cyber Security events (IR-1b)

name: SA-2E: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Indicators of anomalous activity have been defined and are monitored across the operational environment

name: SA-2F: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Monitoring activities are aligned with the function's threat profile (TVM-1d)

name: SA-2G: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Monitoring requirements are based on the risk to the function

name: SA-2H: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Monitoring is integrated with other business and Security processes (e.g., incident response, asset management)

name: SA-2I: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Continuous monitoring is performed across the operational environment to identify anomalous activity

name: SA-2J: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Risk register (RM-2j) content is used to identify indicators of anomalous activity

name: SA-2K: Perform Monitoring
practice_description: Alarms and alerts are configured according to indicators of anomalous activity

name: SA-3A: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
capability: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
practice_description: Methods of communicating the current state of Cyber Security for the function are established and maintained

name: SA-3B: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
capability: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
practice_description: Monitoring data are aggregated to provide an understanding of the operational state of the function (i.e., a common operating picture; a COP may or may not include visualisation or be presented graphically)

name: SA-3C: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
capability: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
practice_description: Information from across the organisation is available to enhance the common operating picture

name: SA-3D: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
capability: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
practice_description: Monitoring data are aggregated to provide near-real-time understanding of the Cyber Security state for the function to enhance the common operating picture

name: SA-3E: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
capability: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
practice_description: Information from outside the organisation is collected to enhance the common operating picture

name: SA-3F: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
capability: Establish and Maintain a Common Operating Picture
practice_description: Predefined states of operation are defined and invoked (manual or automated process) based on the common operating picture

name: SA-AP1: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Operational assets are monitored only for performance and not for cyber security events

name: SA-AP2: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Logging data is only monitored when a cyber security incident occurs

name: SA-AP3: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Normal asset operation is not sufficiently baselined to support the identification of abnormal asset operation

name: SA-AP4: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Alerts and alarms are not configured to include security events

name: SA-AP5: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Logging data is not time synchronised

name: SA-AP6: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Logging data from critical assets is only stored on the asset and not centralised

name: SA-AP7: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Identities (users) have edit (write) access to centralised logging data without a confirmed need

name: SA-AP8: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Third party vendors or services have privileged access that is not logged

name: SA-AP9: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) are only monitored and considered during or after a cyber security incident

name: SA-AP10: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Logging data from impacted assets cannot be inspected when investigating a cyber security event

name: SA-AP11: SA Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Indicators of Compromise cannot be added to security monitoring solutions that monitor critical assets

name: TVM-1A: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: Information sources to support threat management activities are identified, at least in an ad hoc manner.

name: TVM-1B: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: Cyber Security threat information is gathered and interpreted for the function, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: TVM-1C: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: Threats that are considered important to the function are addressed (e.g., implement mitigating controls, monitor threat status), at least in an ad hoc manner

name: TVM-1D: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: A threat profile for the function is established that includes characterisation of likely intent, capability, and target of threats to the function

name: TVM-1E: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: Threat information sources that address all components of the threat profile are prioritised and monitored

name: TVM-1F: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: Identified threats are analysed and prioritised

name: TVM-1G: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: Threats are addressed according to the assigned priority

name: TVM-1H: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: The threat profile for the function is validated at an organisation-defined frequency

name: TVM-1I: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: Analysis and prioritisation of threats are informed by the function's (or organisation's) risk criteria (RM-1c)

name: TVM-1J: Identify and Respond to Threats
practice_description: Threat information is added to the risk register (RM-2j)

name: TVM-2A: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Information sources to support Cyber Security vulnerability discovery are identified (e.g. industry associations, vendors, federal briefings, internal assessments), at least in an ad hoc manner

name: TVM-2B: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Cyber Security vulnerability information is gathered and interpreted for the function, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: TVM-2C: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Cyber Security vulnerabilities that are considered important to the function are addressed (e.g., implement mitigating controls, apply Cyber Security patches), at least in an ad hoc manner

name: TVM-2D: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Cyber Security vulnerability information sources that address all assets important to the function are monitored

name: TVM-2E: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Cyber Security vulnerability assessments are performed (e.g., architectural reviews, penetration testing, Cyber Security exercises, vulnerability identification tools)

name: TVM-2F: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Identified Cyber Security vulnerabilities are analysed and prioritised

name: TVM-2G: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Cyber Security vulnerabilities are addressed according to the assigned priority

name: TVM-2H: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Operational impact to the function is evaluated prior to deploying Cyber Security patches

name: TVM-2I: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Cyber Security vulnerability assessments are performed for all assets important to the delivery of the function, at an organisation-defined frequency

name: TVM-2J: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Cyber Security vulnerability assessments are informed by the function's (or organisation's) risk criteria (RM-1c)

name: TVM-2K: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Cyber Security vulnerability assessments are performed by parties that are independent of the operations of the function

name: TVM-2L: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Analysis and prioritisation of Cyber Security vulnerabilities are informed by the function's (or organisation's) risk criteria (RM-1c)

name: TVM-2M: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Cyber Security vulnerability information is added to the risk register (RM-2j)

name: TVM-2N: Reduce Cyber Security Vulnerabilities
practice_description: Risk monitoring activities validate the responses to Cyber Security vulnerabilities (e.g., deployment of patches or other activities)

name: TVM-AP1: TVM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Where technical or business reasons restrict the ability to remediate an identified vulnerability, no mitigating or compensating controls are investigated and applied

name: TVM-AP2: TVM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Internet-facing assets are not periodically assessed for cyber security vulnerabilities

name: TVM-AP3: TVM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Controls are not updated in response to new and emerging high priority cyber threats

name: WM-1A: Assign Cyber Security Responsibilities
practice_description: Cyber Security responsibilities for the function are identified, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: WM-1B: Assign Cyber Security Responsibilities
practice_description: Cyber Security responsibilities are assigned to specific people, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: WM-1C: Assign Cyber Security Responsibilities
practice_description: Cyber Security responsibilities are assigned to specific roles, including external service providers

name: WM-1D: Assign Cyber Security Responsibilities
practice_description: Cyber Security responsibilities are documented (e.g., in position descriptions)

name: WM-1E: Assign Cyber Security Responsibilities
practice_description: Cyber Security responsibilities and job requirements are reviewed and updated as appropriate

name: WM-1F: Assign Cyber Security Responsibilities
practice_description: Cyber Security responsibilities are included in job performance evaluation criteria

name: WM-1G: Assign Cyber Security Responsibilities
practice_description: Assigned Cyber Security responsibilities are managed to ensure adequacy and redundancy of coverage

name: WM-2A: Control the Workforce Life Cycle
practice_description: Personnel vetting (e.g., background checks, drug tests) is performed, at least in an ad hoc manner, at hire for positions that have access to the assets required for delivery of the function

name: WM-2B: Control the Workforce Life Cycle
practice_description: Personnel termination procedures address Cyber Security, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: WM-2C: Control the Workforce Life Cycle
practice_description: Personnel vetting is performed at an organisation-defined frequency for positions that have access to the assets required for delivery of the function

name: WM-2D: Control the Workforce Life Cycle
practice_description: Personnel transfer procedures address Cyber Security

name: WM-2E: Control the Workforce Life Cycle
practice_description: Risk designations are assigned to all positions that have access to the assets required for delivery of the function

name: WM-2F: Control the Workforce Life Cycle
practice_description: Vetting is performed for all positions (including employees, vendors, and contractors) at a level commensurate with position risk designation

name: WM-2G: Control the Workforce Life Cycle
practice_description: Succession planning is performed for personnel based on risk designation

name: WM-2H: Control the Workforce Life Cycle
practice_description: A formal accountability process that includes disciplinary actions is implemented for personnel who fail to comply with established Security policies and procedures

name: WM-3A: Develop Cyber Security Workforce
practice_description: Cyber Security training is made available, at least in an ad hoc manner, to personnel with assigned Cyber Security responsibilities

name: WM-3B: Develop Cyber Security Workforce
practice_description: Cyber Security knowledge, skill, and ability gaps are identified

name: WM-3C: Develop Cyber Security Workforce
practice_description: Identified gaps are addressed through recruiting and/or training

name: WM-3D: Develop Cyber Security Workforce
practice_description: Cyber Security training is provided as a prerequisite to granting access to assets that support the delivery of the function (e.g., new personnel training, personnel transfer training)

name: WM-3E: Develop Cyber Security Workforce
practice_description: Cyber Security workforce management objectives that support current and future operational needs are established and maintained

name: WM-3F: Develop Cyber Security Workforce
practice_description: Recruiting and retention are aligned to support Cyber Security workforce management objectives

name: WM-3G: Develop Cyber Security Workforce
practice_description: Training programs are aligned to support Cyber Security workforce management objectives

name: WM-3H: Develop Cyber Security Workforce
practice_description: The effectiveness of training programs is evaluated at an organisation-defined frequency and improvements are made as appropriate

name: WM-3I: Develop Cyber Security Workforce
practice_description: Training programs include continuing education and professional development opportunities for personnel with significant Cyber Security responsibilities

name: WM-4A: Increase Cyber Security Awareness
practice_description: Cyber Security awareness activities occur, at least in an ad hoc manner

name: WM-4B: Increase Cyber Security Awareness
practice_description: Objectives for Cyber Security awareness activities are established and maintained

name: WM-4C: Increase Cyber Security Awareness
practice_description: Cyber Security awareness content is based on the organisation's threat profile (TVM-1d)

name: WM-4D: Increase Cyber Security Awareness
practice_description: Cyber Security awareness activities are aligned with the predefined states of operation (SA-3f)

name: WM-4E: Increase Cyber Security Awareness
practice_description: The effectiveness of Cyber Security awareness activities is evaluated at an organisation-defined frequency and improvements are made as appropriate

name: WM-AP1: WM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Cyber security capabilities are dependent on one or two key personnel and no succession plan is in place to ensure retention of critical knowledge

name: WM-AP2: WM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Personnel interacting with critical assets are not assigned additional cyber security responsibilities

name: WM-AP3: WM Anti-Patterns
practice_description: Personnel interacting with critical assets are not aware of their additional assigned cyber security responsibilities (WM-AP2)
